resources.string:
This app has no UI of its own. It registers one Locus compute-track (routing) provider — a bound service Locus calls to turn your via-points into a route.

It is a deliberately "dumb" router: between points it draws a wavy, wiggly line instead of following roads — on purpose, so you can see at a glance the route was computed by a provider and isn't just a straight line. Don't navigate by it.

What it demonstrates:
• reporting capabilities (attribution, supported profiles, via-point count) over AIDL;
• building the route geometry as a list of locations;
• emitting turn-by-turn instructions as waypoints (turn action + index into the geometry).

To try it:
1. Install Locus Map on this device.
2. In Locus, open the route planner / navigation and choose the routing source.
3. Pick "Wavy sample router" and plan a route between two or more points.

The whole provider lives in WavyRouteService.kt — small on purpose. Copy this module as the starting point for a real router (GraphHopper, BRouter, an online service, …): keep the service shell and replace the geometry-and-maneuver computation in computeTrack().
